threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newbie by their struggle with ordering a pint at the Crooked Billet—'I'll have a lager, please' raises eyebrows.
Last night on the 45, a guy serenaded us all with an old Skatalites tune. Brixton magic at 2 AM, complete with impromptu dancing in the aisles.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a guy in a tutu juggling while the bus was late—classic Neukölln. Only here do you find improv art in the middle of chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see people casually sipping beers in the park while their dogs dig through leftover currywurst wrappers—best unique combo!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they pronounce ‘Dolores’—if it’s ‘dough-lore-ess,’ they’re just passing through. We know it’s ‘duh-lore-is.’

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling while waiting for the 19 bus at Taylor and Eddy. Guess he wanted to distract everyone from the 30-minute delay!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the trendy bars, but the quiet afternoons in Plaça de la Vila are where the real local life happens.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still marvel at the Parc Güell's mosaics and snap photos with every step, oblivious to the locals passing by.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: Ordering a 'flat white' at the café and assuming they know what you're talking about. Real locals just call it coffee.
Last night on the 199, a guy was passionately explaining the finer points of cat ownership to a bemused group. Catford really does bring out the quirkiest conversations.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a toddler in a Spiderman costume riding a bicycle while their dog pulled a tiny cart. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a club that serves breakfast until 5 PM, where the techno beats and the smell of fresh croissants coexist.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who’s local by how they navigate BART delays—most know to avoid Civic Center entirely when there's a loud bang.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus with a guy passionately explaining quantum physics to a confused dog. Just another day in the TL, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the bars close early here, but the locals just know the best spots that stay open late, like La Bodega. It's a hidden gem.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a newcomer by their bewildered look while waiting at the wrong tram stop, clutching a guidebook like it’s a map to Narnia.
